$description: ISA-95 UNS via NATS — dot-separated subject mirror of sync/mqtt/isa95-uns-walker-reynolds.json. Same 7-segment hierarchy and same per-attribute payload, only the transport differs. NATS wildcards: '*' = single token, '>' = remaining tokens. Subjects are normalized to lowercase to follow NATS convention; the segment values themselves remain case-preserving so downstream KG can still distinguish 'SGM-004' from 'sgm-004' if needed.

nats.$note: leafNode=true means this Edge IPC connects via NATS Leaf Node which forwards subjects to the central cluster. Connector publishes to nats://nats:4222 inside the IPC compose network.

domain.description: Data domain: BDE, ProcessData, TMS, Maintenance, Energy

attribute.description: Attribute name (flattened, sub-categories joined with underscore)

headers.$description: NATS supports message headers (since 2.2). i3x sets these on every publish for fast-path consumer routing without payload parsing.

machineIdResolution.description: Segment 4 is the machine ID, resolved against SM Profile kgIdProperty 'machine_id'

BDE.description: Betriebsdatenerfassung — real-time production counters and status

TMS.description: Tool Management — tool ID, wear, lifetime

Maintenance.description: Maintenance signals — running hours, alerts, service-due

ERP.description: ERP entities (site-scoped, not machine-scoped) — Articles, Orders, Customers, Suppliers, BOM, Stock
ERP.$note: 5-segment ERP namespace, parallel to the 7-segment machine namespace.

QMS.description: Quality entities (site-scoped) — InspectionLot, InspectionResult, QualityNotification, SPC, CorrectiveAction

compatibility.$description: Semantic equivalence with sync/mqtt/isa95-uns-walker-reynolds.json. A bridge can translate slash-topics to dot-subjects and vice versa.
